name: Tex-Mex Turkey Chili - Southern
recipeIngredient: 1/4 cup extra sharp shredded cheese (cheddar, jack, optional)
recipeInstructions:
1. Slice the turkey thighs away from the bones on a chopping board. Cut the meat into 1-inch cubes and discard the skin.
2. Spray a non-stick pot or pressure cooker with cooking spray for no-fat frying.
3. Add the cubed meat to the pot and brown over moderate heat in its own melted fat (no added oil needed).
4. Remove from the flame and stir boiling water into the juices in the pot.
5. Pour the liquid into a cup, set aside until fat rises to the surface.
6. With a bulb-type baster, skim off and discard the surface fat.
7. Return the fat-skimmed liquid to the pot (bones may be added for flavor and removed before serving).
8. Add all remaining ingredients except cheese. Cover and simmer over low heat until tender, about 1 hour in a conventional pot or 20 minutes in a pressure cooker.
9. Uncover and continue to simmer until most of the liquid evaporates and the chili is thick.
10. Spoon into a serving dish and top with cheese.
